content.problemStatement:
Salmon loves to be a tidy person. One day, when he looked at the mess that he made after playing with his rubber ducks, he felt awful. Now he wants to clean up his mess, by placing his ducks into boxes.

Each rubber duck has a color. There are a total of N+1N+1N+1 colors, numbered from 000 to NNN. Salmon wants to place his Nâˆ—KN*KNâˆ—K ducks into NNN boxes, each of which can fit KKK ducks. Each duck should be placed inside a box.

Salmon is very particular when it comes to how many colors he can place in each box. Since Salmon doesn't like lots of colors mixing together he only wants to have a maximum of 222 distinct colors per box.

Please help Salmon achieve this goal!

It can be shown that there will always be at least one valid solution under given constraints.

If there are multiple correct solutions, you may output any one of them.

Input:
The first line contains an integer TTT, denoting the number of testcases. TTT testcases will follow, each containing two lines.
The first line of each testcase contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line of each testcase contains N+1N+1N+1  space-separated integers. The i+1i+1i+1-th integer denotes the number of ducks with color-iii where 0â‰¤iâ‰¤N0 \leq i \leq N0â‰¤iâ‰¤N
Output:
Output NNN lines for each testcase.
The iii-th line of a testcase should contain 444 space-separated integers c1,m1,c2,m2c1, m1, c2, m2c1,m1,c2,m2 respectively which denotes that that are m1m1m1 ducks of color-c1c1c1 and m2m2m2 ducks of color-c2c2c2 inside the iii-th box where 0â‰¤m1,m2â‰¤K0 \leq m1,m2 \leq K0â‰¤m1,m2â‰¤K and 0â‰¤c1,c2â‰¤N0 \leq c1,c2 \leq N0â‰¤c1,c2â‰¤N.
Note that even if you have only one color to put inside the iii-th box, you should still output 444 space-separated integers and keep either m1m1m1 or m2m2m2 as 000. And 0â‰¤c1,c2â‰¤N0 \leq c1,c2 \leq N0â‰¤c1,c2â‰¤N.
The output should be valid and should satisfy Salmon's goal.
constraints: Total ducks for each test case is exactly Nâˆ—KN*KNâˆ—K
There can be a color with 000 ducks
In the given testcase, Salmon has N=2N=2N=2 boxes, each of size K=4K=4K=4 and there are total Nâˆ—K=8N*K=8Nâˆ—K=8 ducks.
The first box is filled with 111 duck of color-222 and 333 ducks of color-111 and the second box is filled with 111 duck of color-111 and 333 ducks of color-000.
Each duck is inside a box and each box has at most two distinct colors. Also each box contains exactly K=4K=4K=4 ducks.
